recap: England withstood a dramatic second-half collapse to beat France 6-4 in a chaotic third-place playoff, with Bukayo Saka's hat-trick—including a stoppage-time penalty—proving decisive after the hosts led 4-0. Kylian Mbappé's two goals moved him past Lionel Messi's career World Cup record with 22 total, and Bradley Barcola's strike pulled France within one at 4-3, but England's defense held firm despite sustained pressure. The 10-goal spectacle marked the highest-scoring third-place match ever and the most goals in a World Cup game since Hungary's 10-1 demolition of El Salvador in 1982.
